steps.title: Identify the goal
steps.content: The student wants to CONTRAST the two tile styles. A successful answer must put both styles side-by-side and highlight how they differ, drawing on the defining detail from the notes: majolica designs are painted directly on the tile, while arista designs are stamped into the tile.

steps.title: Find the choice that delivers the contrast
steps.content: Only one option states both methods and uses a contrast connector ("whereas") to set them against each other. The others either name both styles without distinguishing them or describe just one.

steps.title: Eliminate per choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'can be found in the Royal Alcazar': tells where the tiles are located; no contrast of styles. ✗
  • Choice B — 'famous for its intricate tilework': mentions both styles but only describes the palace's fame, not how the styles differ. ✗
  • Choice C — 'stamped into the ceramic tiles, whereas... painted directly': explicitly contrasts the two defining techniques. ✓
  • Choice D — 'majolica style tiles, made by painting': describes only majolica; no contrast with arista. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
